elements.sent-2411-001: The discovery of high-temperature superconductivity (HTSC) in pressurized La$_3$Ni$_2$O$_7$ has aroused a surge in the exploration of HTSC in the multilayer nickelates.
elements.sent-2411-002: Presently, while HTSC is only found in pressurized circumstance, most of the experimental detections are performed at ambient pressure (AP) due to technical problems.
elements.sent-2411-003: Here we focus on the single-bilayer film of La$_3$Ni$_2$O$_7$ at AP, and propose that an imposed strong perpendicular electric field can strongly enhance its superconducting $T_c$.
elements.sent-2411-005: Under strong electric field, the layer with lower potential energy will accept electrons flowing from the other layer to fill in the Ni-$3d_{x^2-y^2}$ orbitals in this layer, as the nearly half-filled Ni-$3d_{z^2}$ orbital in this layer cannot accommodate more electrons.
elements.sent-2411-006: With the enhancement of the filling fraction in the $3d_{x^2-y^2}$ orbitals in this layer, the interlayer s-wave pairing will be subjected to the pair-breaking effect and be suppressed, but the intralayer d-wave pairing in this layer is promptly and strongly enhanced, which mimics the cuprates.
elements.sent-2411-007: Our combined simplified one-orbital study and comprehensive two-orbital one under mean-field treatment consistently verify this idea and yield that an imposed voltage of about $0.1\sim0.2$ volt between the two layers is enough to get the HTSC at AP.
elements.sent-2411-008: Our results appeal for experimental verification.
elements.sent-2411-009: Introduction: The discovery of superconductivity (SC) with critical temperature $T_c\approx 80$ K in the pressurized La$_3$Ni$_2$O$_7$~\cite{Wang2023LNO, YuanHQ2023LNO,Wang2023LNOb,wang2023LNOpoly,wang2023la2prnio7,zhang2023pressure,zhou2023evidence,wang2024bulk,li2024pressure has attracted great interests \cite{Fukamachi2001,khasanov2024pressure,chen2024evidence,dan2024spin,chen2024electronic,Wang2022LNO,Kakoi2024,xie2024neutron,gupta2024anisotropic,feng2024unaltered,meng2024density,fan2024tunn,xu2024pressure,LI2024distinct,liu2024electronic .}}
elements.sent-2411-010: This discovery has aroused exploration of high-$T_c$ SC (HTSC) in multilayer nickelates, resulting in the discovery of SC in the pressurized La$_4$Ni$_3$O$_{10}$~\cite{zhu2024superconductivity,zhang2023superconductivity,huang2024signature,li2023trilayer,zhang2020intertwined,xu2024origin,du2024correlated , which in together with the previously synthesized infinite-layer nickelates Nd$_{1-x}$Sr$_x$NiO$_2$~\cite{li2019superconductivity,lee2023linear,nomura2022superconductivity,gu2022superconductivity have established a new superconductors family other than the cuprates and the iron-based superconductors, arousing lots of studies~\cite{yang2024orbital,wang2023structure,cui2023strain,sui2023rno,YaoDX2023,Dagotto2023,cao2023flat,zhang2023structural,huang2023impurity,geisler2023structural,rhodes2023structural,zhang2023la3ni2o6,yuan2023trilayer,li2024la3,geisler2024optical,li2017fermiology,wang2024non,Li2024ele,li2024distinguishing,zhou2024revealing,wang2024chemical,chen2024tri,Chen2024poly,Dong2024vis,Li2024design,puphal2024unconven, ZhangGM2023DMRG,Werner2023,shilenko2023correlated,WuWei2023charge,chen2023critical,ouyang2023hund,heier2023competing,wang2024electronic,botzel2024theory,WangQH2023,YangF2023,lechermann2023,Kuroki2023,HuJP2023,lu2023bilayertJ,oh2023type2,liao2023electron,qu2023bilayer,Yi_Feng2023,jiang2023high,zhang2023trends,qin2023high,tian2023correlation,jiang2023pressure,lu2023sc,kitamine2023,luo2023high,zhang2023strong,pan2023rno,sakakibara2023La4Ni3O10,lange2023mixedtj,yang2023strong,lange2023feshbach,kaneko2023pair,fan2023sc,wu2024deconfined,zhang2024prediction,zhang2024s,Yang2024effective,zhang2024electronic,yang2024decom,ryee2024quenched,Lu2024interplay,Ouyang2024absence,labollita2024,zhang2024emergent,Leonov2024Electronic,labollita2024assessing,ni2024first,Yi2024nature,LaBollita2024electronic,jiang2024theory,chen2024non,zhang2024magnetic,lin2024magnetic,qin2024intertwined,Leonov2024Electronicc .}}}
elements.sent-2411-011: Presently, although various groups have conducted experiments to investigate the properties of La$_3$Ni$_2$O$_7$~\cite{Fukamachi2001,khasanov2024pressure,chen2024evidence,dan2024spin,chen2024electronic,Wang2022LNO,Kakoi2024,xie2024neutron,gupta2024anisotropic,feng2024unaltered,meng2024density,fan2024tunn,xu2024pressure,LI2024distinct,liu2024electronic,Fukamachi2001 , most of these experiments were conducted at ambient pressure (AP).}
elements.sent-2411-012: The circumstance of high pressure not only strongly hinders the experimental detection of the samples, but also brings difficulties in the application of the SC in industry.
elements.sent-2411-013: Therefore, it is eagerly expected that HTSC can be realized in the multilayer nickelates at AP.
elements.sent-2411-014: Here we propose a viable approach to realize HTSC in La$_3$Ni$_2$O$_7$ at AP.
elements.sent-2411-015: Presently, the pairing mechanism in the pressurized La$_3$Ni$_2$O$_7$ is still under debate~\cite{WangQH2023,YangF2023,lechermann2023,Kuroki2023,HuJP2023,lu2023bilayertJ,oh2023type2,liao2023electron,qu2023bilayer,Yi_Feng2023,jiang2023high,zhang2023trends,qin2023high,tian2023correlation,jiang2023pressure,lu2023sc,kitamine2023,luo2023high,zhang2023strong,pan2023rno,sakakibara2023La4Ni3O10,lange2023mixedtj,yang2023strong,lange2023feshbach,kaneko2023pair,fan2023sc,wu2024deconfined,zhang2024electronic,yang2024decom,ryee2024quenched,Lu2024interplay,Ouyang2024absence .}
elements.sent-2411-016: Density-functional-theory (DFT) based first-principle calculations have suggested that the low-energy orbitals are mainly Ni-$3d_{z^2}$ and $3d_{x^2-y^2}$, which are nearly half- and quarter- filled~\cite{Wang2023LNO,YaoDX2023,Dagotto2023 .}
elements.sent-2411-017: Due to the various experiments which have revealed the strongly-correlated characteristic of the material~\cite{liu2024electronic, yang2024orbital , we can take a strong-coupling viewpoint of the system.}
elements.sent-2411-018: In this viewpoint, the strong Hubbard repulsion suppresses the coherent motion of the nearly half-filled $3d_{z^2}$ electrons, which can almost be viewed as localized spins.
elements.sent-2411-019: Therefore, the main carrier of SC in the material should be the $3d_{x^2-y^2}$ electrons, which subject to the in-plane superexchange interaction just mimics the 50\% hole-doped cuprates.
elements.sent-2411-020: However, the $3d_{z^2}$ orbitals also play an important role through interplaying with the $3d_{x^2-y^2}$ orbitals.
elements.sent-2411-021: The strong interlayer superexchange of the $3d_{z^2}$ electrons~\cite{ZhangGM2023DMRG provides the pairing potential, which can be transmitted to the $3d_{x^2-y^2}$ electrons through the Hund's rule~\cite{lu2023bilayertJ,oh2023type2,qu2023bilayer,zhang2023strong or the nearest-neighbor (NN) hybridization~\cite{ZhangGM2023DMRG,Yi_Feng2023 or both.}}}
elements.sent-2411-022: In such viewpoint, the role of pressure in enhancing the $T_c$ may lie in the enhancement of the interlayer superexchange, the inter-orbital hybridization, or their combination.
elements.sent-2411-023: At AP, the enhancement of these parameters might be realized through chemical element substitution or chemical doping, which is still on the way.
elements.figure-2411-001: \includegraphics[width=1\linewidth]{sd.png} \caption{(a) Schematic diagram for the dominant hopping integrals and superexchange interactions between the $E_g$ orbitals in La$_3$Ni$_2$O$_7$. (b) Schematic diagram illustrating that the Hund's rule coupling transmits the interlayer superexchange interaction $J_\perp$ between the $3d_{z^2}$ orbitals to the effective one $\tilde{J}_\perp$ between the $3d_{x^2-y^2}$ orbitals.} \label{fig_SD}
elements.sent-2411-024: In this work, we propose an alternative approach to realize HTSC in La$_3$Ni$_2$O$_7$ at AP.
elements.sent-2411-025: We focus on the thin film of this material.
elements.sent-2411-026: The La$_3$Ni$_2$O$_7$ film has been synthesized, in which the resonant soft x-ray experiment has revealed spin density wave (SDW)~\cite{gupta2024anisotropic .}
elements.sent-2411-027: Here we consider the thinnest limit, i.e. a single bilayer film of La$_3$Ni$_2$O$_7$.
elements.sent-2411-028: The single bilayer film can be grown by using such approaches as the molecular beam epitaxy, the chemical vapor deposition or the pulsed laser deposition.
elements.sent-2411-029: We can impose a perpendicular electric field, say pointing upward, in this single bilayer, so that electrons from the top layer will flow to the bottom layer.
elements.sent-2411-030: As the $3d_{z^2}$ orbitals in the bottom layer is already half-filled and thus cannot accommodate more electrons due to the strong Hubbard repulsion, the electrons which flow to the bottom layer have to fill the $3d_{x^2-y^2}$ orbitals.
elements.sent-2411-031: Such enhancement of the filling fraction of the bottom-layer $3d_{x^2-y^2}$ orbitals will first suppress the interlayer s-wave SC due to mismatch of the electron numbers between the two layers, and then promptly lead to the intralayer d-wave HTSC in the bottom layer.
elements.sent-2411-032: To test this idea, we have performed combined simplified single-orbital study and comprehensive two-orbital one, with both studies consistently verify that an voltage of around $0.1\sim 0.2$ volt between the two layers is enough to induce d-wave HTSC in the bottom layer.
elements.sent-2411-033: Interestingly, the d-wave SC carried by the $3d_{x^2-y^2}$ electrons in the bottom layer coexists with the interlayer s-wave pseudo-gap carried by the $3d_{z^2}$ electrons.
elements.sent-2411-034: Our proposal potentially provides a viable approach to realize HTSC in the single bilayer film of La$_3$Ni$_2$O$_7$.
elements.sent-2411-035: General Consideration and a Simplified Study: Due to the quasi-2D structure of the material, the lattice and electronic structures of the single-bilayer La$_3$Ni$_2$O$_7$ film should be near those of the bulk material.
elements.sent-2411-036: The Ni atoms in the AP phase crystal approximately form a bilayer square lattice.
elements.sent-2411-037: As illustrated in Fig.~\ref{fig_SD (a), the leading hopping integrals are the interlayer hopping of the $d_{z^2}$ electrons $t_{\perp}$ and the intralayer nearest-neighbor (NN) hopping of the $d_{x^2-y^2}$ electrons $t_{\parallel}$.}
elements.sent-2411-038: Under strong Hubbard $U$, these hopping terms can induce the effective superexchange interaction $J_{\perp}$ and $J_{\parallel}$ through the relation $J\approx \frac{4t^2 {U}$.}
elements.sent-2411-039: Under the Hund's rule coupling $J_H$, the spins of the two orbitals are inclined to be parallel aligned, as illustrated in Fig.~\ref{fig_SD (b), which partly transmits the interlayer superexchange $J_{\perp}$ between the $d_{z^2}$ orbitals to the $d_{x^2-y^2}$ orbitals as $\tilde{J _{\perp =\alpha J_{\perp}$ with $\alpha\in (0,1)$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-040: In addition, there exists intralayer NN-bond hybridization $t_{xz}$ between the two orbitals.
elements.sent-2411-041: As shown in Fig.~\ref{fig_idea (a), the quarter-filled $3d_{x^2-y^2}$ electrons subject to the $J_{\parallel}$ and the $\tilde{J _{\perp}$ terms can pair in principle.}}

elements.sent-2411-043: Now let us turn on the perpendicular electric field $\bm{\varepsilon}$ pointing upward, as shown in Fig.~\ref{fig_idea (c) and (d).}
elements.sent-2411-044: Under this field, the electrons flow from the top layer to the bottom one.
elements.sent-2411-045: As the $3d_{z^2}$ orbitals in the bottom layer is already nearly half-filled which cannot accommodate more electrons, the majority of the electrons flowing to this layer will fill in the $3d_{x^2-y^2}$ orbitals.
elements.sent-2411-046: In the top layer, since the $3d_{z^2}$ orbitals host larger density of state (DOS) than the $3d_{x^2-y^2}$ orbitals, they will donate most electrons.
elements.sent-2411-047: These donated electrons can flow to the $3d_{x^2-y^2}$ orbitals in the two layers, with more of them to the $3d_{x^2-y^2}$ orbitals in the bottom layer.
elements.sent-2411-048: Even with doped holes under the electric field, the top-layer $3d_{z^2}$ electrons still cannot carry SC: Firstly, lacking pairing interaction, they cannot form intralayer pairing.
elements.sent-2411-049: Secondly, although they can pair with the localized bottom-layer $3d_{z^2}$ electrons, such pair cannot coherently move, only resulting in pseudo-gap.
elements.sent-2411-050: Therefore, the SC in the presence of $\bm{\varepsilon}$ can only be carried by the $3d_{x^2-y^2}$ orbitals.
elements.sent-2411-051: As the filling fractions of the $3d_{x^2-y^2}$ orbitals in the two layers are different, their Fermi levels are relatively shift, which will suppress their interlayer pairing.
elements.sent-2411-052: The $3d_{x^2-y^2}$ orbitals in the bottom layer will form intralayer d-wave SC, mimicsing the cuprates, as shown in Fig.~\ref{fig_idea (d).}
elements.sent-2411-053: When the field $\bm{\varepsilon}$ is strong enough so that the filling fraction of the bottom $3d_{x^2-y^2}$ orbitals is near that of the optimal doped cuprates, d-wave HTSC will be achieved in the material.

elements.sent-2411-104: Conclusion and Discussion: In conclusion, we propose that an imposed strong perpendicular electric field can enhance the superconducting $T_c$ of the single-bilayer film of La$_3$Ni$_2$O$_7$ at AP.
elements.sent-2411-105: The reason lies in that under the strong electric field, the electrons in the layer with higher potential energy will flow to the layer with lower potential energy, to fill the $3d_{x^2-y^2}$ orbitals in the latter layer.
elements.sent-2411-106: With considerably enhanced filling fraction, the $3d_{x^2-y^2}$ electrons in that layer just mimics the cuprates, which form intralayer d-wave HTSC.
elements.sent-2411-107: Our combined one-orbital and two-orbital studies consistently verify this idea.
elements.sent-2411-108: Our results predict that when the imposed voltage between the two layers is stronger than $0.1\sim 0.2$ eV, the resulted $T_c$ can go beyond the boiling point of liquid nitrogen.
elements.sent-2411-109: In our calculations, we have adopted the TB parameters from the DFT calculations for bulk La$_3$Ni$_2$O$_7$ at AP, which might be slightly different from those for the single-bilayer film.
elements.sent-2411-110: However, the strong-coupling calculations performed here do not seriously rely on the accurate values of these parameters, because the main physics here is very simple.
elements.sent-2411-111: Actually, the well consistency between the result of the comprehensive two-orbital study and those of the simplified one-orbital studies with assuming different input conditions just verifies the robustness of our conclusion.
